summary_one_line: A valid non-palette PNG carrying a tRNS chunk (num_trans!=0) under the PNG_EXPAND transformation, requiring exact chunk-type/length bytes.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid PNG that parses past IHDR into a non-palette (RGB, color_type=2) image and carries a tRNS chunk so that png_ptr->num_trans becomes non-zero, reaching the true side of line 1967 inside the else (non-palette) arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are cmplog-discovered, well-formed PNGs (correct magic 89 50 4e 47 0d 0a 1a 0a, IHDR, gAMA, sRGB chunks) with IHDR color_type byte = 02 (RGB) held at offset 0x19 across all 10 winners, routing into the else branch; the blocker true side fired T=10 F=0 for W meaning num_trans was set non-zero by a successfully-parsed tRNS chunk.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser (naive) seeds at offset 0x19 carry 00 (x8) and 04 (x2) instead of the winner's 02 RGB color_type, and corrupt the structural bytes (e.g. offset 0x1d shows R=52 in all winners but 7f/00/ff in losers), so parsing never establishes a non-palette image with a valid tRNS chunk; the blocker fired T=0 F=10 for L (num_trans stayed 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparand operands (PNG chunk-type FOURCCs and length/CRC fields compared during chunk parsing) into the input at the matching byte offsets, letting cmplog construct a valid tRNS chunk and the RGB color_type that naive's blind havoc mutators cannot reliably reproduce.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the true side of `if (png_ptr->num_trans != 0)`, gating on a tRNS chunk having set num_trans.
The blocker sits in the else (non-palette) arm of the PNG_EXPAND handling.
All winner seeds hold IHDR color_type byte = 02 (RGB) at offset 0x19 while losers hold 00/04.
Winner seeds are valid PNGs discovered by cmplog with correct magic and chunk structure.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=230924cbdaa2cc5c, size=8774 bytes, fuzzer=cmplog, trial=1, discovered_at=18s

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds corrupt structural chunk bytes; offset 0x1d is R=52 in all winners but 7f/00/ff in losers.
The blocker fired true 10/10 for winner seeds and false 10/10 for loser seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1967  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (png_ptr->num_trans != 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability, only blind havoc/token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3915 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 230924cbdaa2cc5c` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the 4-level ancestor chain; the tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 3', the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the tRNS-presence gate depends only on a magic chunk-length/FOURCC comparison shows cmplog and naive resolving the branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, indicating I2S substitution gives no advantage for reaching num_trans!=0.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff evidence ties the divergence to chunk-structure and color_type bytes, but it does not pin the specific tRNS chunk-type/length comparand that I2S substitutes, so the exact CMP operand driving the win is inferred rather than directly observed.
